name: curl
description: Transfer data from or to a server using supported protocols

name: -X
--request
options.description: HTTP method to use (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, PATCH, etc.)

name: -H
--header
options.description: Pass custom header to server

name: -d
--data
options.description: Send specified data in a POST request

name: --data-binary
options.description: Send data exactly as specified, preserving newlines and carriage returns

name: --data-raw
options.description: Send data as-is without special interpretation of @ prefix

name: --data-urlencode
options.description: URL-encode the data before sending

name: -F
--form
options.description: Specify multipart form data

name: -G
--get
options.description: Put the post data in the URL and use GET

name: -o
--output
options.description: Write output to file instead of stdout

name: -O
--remote-name
options.description: Write output to file named like the remote file

name: -T
--upload-file
options.description: Transfer local file to destination URL

name: -L
--location
options.description: Follow redirects

name: -v
--verbose
options.description: Make the operation more talkative

name: -s
--silent
options.description: Silent mode — no progress meter or error messages

name: -S
--show-error
options.description: Show error even when -s is used

name: -f
--fail
options.description: Fail silently on HTTP errors (no output on server errors)

name: -I
--head
options.description: Fetch headers only

name: -k
--insecure
options.description: Allow insecure server connections when using SSL

name: -u
--user
options.description: Server user and password (user:password)

name: -b
--cookie
options.description: Send cookies from string or file

name: -c
--cookie-jar
options.description: Write cookies to file after operation

name: -A
--user-agent
options.description: Send User-Agent header

name: -e
--referer
options.description: Send Referer URL header

name: -w
--write-out
options.description: Output format after transfer completes

name: --compressed
options.description: Request compressed response and decompress it

name: --connect-timeout
options.description: Maximum time allowed for connection phase in seconds

name: -m
--max-time
options.description: Maximum time allowed for the transfer in seconds

name: --retry
options.description: Retry request if transient problems occur

name: -x
--proxy
options.description: Use specified proxy

name: --cert
options.description: Client certificate file and password

name: --key
options.description: Private key file name

name: --cacert
options.description: CA certificate file to verify peer against

name: --http1.1
options.description: Use HTTP 1.1

name: --http2
options.description: Use HTTP/2

name: --http3
options.description: Use HTTP/3

name: --ipv4
options.description: Resolve names to IPv4 addresses only

name: --ipv6
options.description: Resolve names to IPv6 addresses only
